codeReview.summary: The AWQ model setup feature correctly implements AWQ model discovery, download, and kernel integration. The implementation satisfies both VAL-AWQ-001 (AWQ model available and loadable) and VAL-AWQ-002 (AWQ kernel in C dispatch path). Minor optimization opportunity exists for gate+up projections which still use zero-point subtraction.

issues.description: AWQ kernel only applied to down_proj, not gate+up projections. The gemv_int4_dual_fused kernel (used for gate+up) still performs zero-point subtraction even though zeros=0 in AWQ. This is functionally correct but misses ~2/3 of the potential AWQ speedup benefit.

Tests skip (return True) when AWQ model is not present. While this is good for CI, it means full integration testing requires manual model download. Consider adding a --skip-missing flag or environment variable to make this behavior more explicit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Kernel selection follows a priority order (AWQ > v5 > v3) documented in code comments. This pattern should be documented in AGENTS.md for future kernel additions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/inference/engine.py:2771-2779 shows priority: if _awq_mode and _gemv_int4_v5_awq → AWQ kernel, elif _gemv_int4_v5 → v5, else fallback

sharedStateObservations.observation: AWQ vs GPTQ quantization difference well documented: AWQ uses w = q * scale (no zero-point) while GPTQ uses w = (q - zero) * scale. This eliminates 8 v_sub_f32 instructions per uint32 word.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: notes/AWQ_INTEGRATION.md provides comprehensive documentation. src/kernels/gemv_int4_v5_awq.hip:15-22 documents the compute savings.

sharedStateObservations.observation: set_awq_mode() must be called BEFORE build_dispatch_cache() for optimal performance. Calling after triggers an automatic cache rebuild with performance warning. This ordering requirement should be more prominently documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/inference/tp_engine.py:2833-2837 shows automatic cache rebuild when called after cache is built. The warning mentions 'rebuilding dispatch cache' but doesn't explain the ordering implication.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Model download requires huggingface-hub package. The download script auto-installs it if missing, but this could be added to services.yaml or a requirements file for pre-installation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: scripts/download_awq_model.sh:24-25 shows: 'pip3 show huggingface-hub > /dev/null 2>&1 || pip3 install huggingface-hub -q'

summary: PASS. The awq-model-setup feature correctly implements all three steps: (1) AWQ model discovery and download script for QuantTrio/Qwen3.5-27B-AWQ, (2) AWQ kernel integration into C dispatch path with proper cache invalidation, (3) comprehensive test coverage for format detection, loader, engine load, and C dispatch plan. Both VAL-AWQ-001 and VAL-AWQ-002 assertions are satisfied. Minor non-blocking issue: AWQ kernel only applied to down_proj projection, leaving optimization opportunity for gate+up projections which could benefit from a future gemv_int4_dual_awq.hip variant.
